• The policy focuses on diversifying export baskets, exploring new markets, and improving supply chain infrastructure for perishable goods.
• It includes provisions for promoting organic and GI-tagged agricultural products, ensuring quality standards, and providing financial incentives to farmers and exporters.
• Technology adoption, including blockchain for traceability and e-commerce platforms, is a key pillar to enhance competitiveness.
• The initiative seeks to empower farmers, boost rural incomes, and establish India as a major global player in agricultural trade.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Food processing and related industries in India- scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
Economics of animal-rearing.
prelimsPoints: Objectives of the new agricultural export policy.
Key features: market diversification, quality standards, incentives.
Role of technology (blockchain, e-commerce) in agri-exports.
Significance of GI-tagged products in exports.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by India in agricultural exports and how the new policy aims to address them.
Analyze the potential impact of promoting organic and GI-tagged products on farmers' income and India's global trade presence.
Examine the role of infrastructure development and technology in enhancing the competitiveness of Indian agricultural produce.
Critically evaluate the policy's effectiveness in achieving the goal of doubling farmers' income.
GS3.imageDescription: A vibrant image of diverse Indian agricultural products, including spices, fruits, and vegetables, arranged for export.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the world's largest producers of several agricultural commodities, yet its share in global agricultural trade remains relatively small, indicating significant untapped potential.

GS3.headline: Indian Cities Adopt Green Infrastructure to Combat Urban Heat Island Effect
GS3.explainer:
• Several major Indian cities are implementing green infrastructure projects, including urban forests, green roofs, and permeable pavements, to mitigate the urban heat island (UHI) effect.
• The UHI effect causes urban areas to be significantly warmer than surrounding rural areas, impacting public health, energy consumption, and air quality.
• Initiatives involve planting climate-resilient trees, developing urban wetlands, and promoting cool roof technologies in building codes.
• These measures aim to reduce ambient temperatures, enhance biodiversity, improve air quality, and provide recreational spaces for residents.
• The projects align with India's smart city mission and climate action plans, focusing on sustainable urban development.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
prelimsPoints: Definition of Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect.
Causes and consequences of UHI.
Examples of green infrastructure solutions (urban forests, green roofs, cool pavements).
Government initiatives like Smart Cities Mission and their role.
mainsPoints: Explain the Urban Heat Island effect and its implications for public health and urban sustainability in Indian cities.
Discuss various green infrastructure solutions being adopted and their effectiveness in mitigating UHI.
Analyze the challenges in implementing sustainable urban planning strategies in rapidly growing Indian cities.
Suggest policy recommendations for integrating climate resilience into urban development plans.
GS3.imageDescription: An aerial view of a modern city showing a mix of concrete structures and newly integrated green spaces like rooftop gardens and tree-lined avenues.
GS3.trivia: Studies show that urban heat islands can raise ambient temperatures by several degrees Celsius, leading to increased energy demand for cooling and higher incidence of heat-related illnesses.

GS3.headline: India Unveils Advanced Indigenous Defence Drone for Surveillance and Reconnaissance
GS3.explainer:
• The Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) unveiled an advanced indigenous Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) designed for high-altitude surveillance and reconnaissance.
• The drone features extended endurance, stealth capabilities, and advanced sensor suites, significantly enhancing India's intelligence gathering and border security capabilities.
• Developed under the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative, this project marks a major milestone in indigenous defence manufacturing and technological self-reliance.
• It is expected to be deployed by the Indian Armed Forces for critical missions, reducing dependency on foreign suppliers.
• The successful development highlights India's growing prowess in aerospace engineering and strategic defence technologies.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.

Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
prelimsPoints: Name/Type of the indigenous drone and its primary functions (surveillance, reconnaissance).
Developer (DRDO).
Significance for 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' in defence.
Applications in border security and intelligence gathering.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of indigenization in defence manufacturing for India's national security and economic growth.
Analyze the role of advanced UAVs in modern warfare and border management, particularly for India.
Evaluate the progress and challenges of the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative in the defence sector.
Examine how technological advancements in defence contribute to India's strategic autonomy.
GS3.imageDescription: A sleek, modern indigenous defence drone with an Indian flag insignia, flying against a clear sky.
GS3.trivia: India is among a select few countries capable of designing and manufacturing advanced military-grade UAVs, a testament to its growing scientific and engineering capabilities.

GS3.headline: India Joins Global Quantum Computing Research Initiative
GS3.explainer:
• India has formally joined a major international collaboration focused on accelerating research and development in quantum computing and related technologies.
• This partnership will facilitate knowledge sharing, joint projects, and access to advanced quantum infrastructure for Indian scientists and researchers.
• The collaboration aims to address complex computational challenges in fields like medicine, material science, and artificial intelligence.
• It aligns with India's National Quantum Mission, which seeks to position the country as a leader in quantum technologies.
• Experts believe this move will boost India's capabilities in a critical emerging technology and foster global scientific diplomacy.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: What is quantum computing and its potential applications.
Purpose of National Quantum Mission.
Benefits of international collaboration in scientific research.
Key concepts: Qubits, entanglement.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of quantum computing for India's technological future and national security.
Analyze the objectives and potential impact of India's National Quantum Mission.
Examine the role of international collaboration in accelerating research and development in cutting-edge technologies like quantum computing.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India in becoming a global leader in quantum technologies.
GS3.imageDescription: An abstract visualization representing quantum computing, with interconnected nodes and glowing lines symbolizing quantum states.
GS3.trivia: Unlike classical computers that use bits (0 or 1), quantum computers use qubits, which can represent 0, 1, or both simultaneously (superposition), allowing for exponentially more powerful computations.

GS3.headline: Government Initiates Mid-Term Review of Foreign Trade Policy 2023
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry has begun a mid-term review of the Foreign Trade Policy (FTP) 2023, seeking feedback from stakeholders.
• The review aims to assess the policy's effectiveness in boosting exports, addressing trade imbalances, and adapting to global economic shifts.
• Focus areas include simplifying export procedures, enhancing logistics infrastructure, and promoting e-commerce exports.
• Stakeholders, including export promotion councils and industry associations, are invited to provide inputs for potential amendments and new incentives.
• The exercise reflects the government's commitment to creating a dynamic and responsive trade ecosystem to achieve ambitious export targets.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: Purpose of Foreign Trade Policy (FTP) and its review.
Key objectives of FTP (boosting exports, addressing imbalances).
Role of Ministry of Commerce and Industry.
Concept of e-commerce exports.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of a robust Foreign Trade Policy for India's economic growth and global integration.
Analyze the challenges faced by Indian exporters and how the FTP aims to provide solutions.
Evaluate the role of policy reviews in adapting to evolving global trade dynamics and domestic economic needs.
Suggest measures to further enhance India's export competitiveness, particularly in the context of global supply chain disruptions.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic depicting global trade routes with India highlighted, symbolizing export and import activities.
GS3.trivia: India's Foreign Trade Policy is usually announced for a period of five years, with mid-term reviews to adjust to changing economic scenarios and provide policy stability to exporters and importers.

GS3.headline: Government Initiates Public Consultation on AI Ethics Framework
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has released a draft framework on Artificial Intelligence (AI) ethics for public consultation.
• The framework aims to ensure responsible development and deployment of AI, addressing concerns related to privacy, bias, accountability, and security.
• It proposes guidelines for data governance, algorithmic transparency, human oversight, and fairness in AI systems across various sectors.
• This initiative is part of India's broader strategy to harness the potential of AI while mitigating its risks and establishing a trustworthy AI ecosystem.
• Feedback from industry, academia, civil society, and the public will be incorporated to finalize the comprehensive policy.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: Purpose of AI ethics framework.
Key principles of responsible AI (privacy, fairness, accountability).
Ministry responsible for AI policy (MeitY).
Challenges posed by AI (bias, security).
mainsPoints: Discuss the ethical challenges and societal implications arising from the rapid advancements in Artificial Intelligence.
Analyze the key provisions of India's proposed AI ethics framework and its potential to foster responsible AI development.
Evaluate the importance of public consultation in shaping policies for emerging technologies like AI.
Suggest measures to balance innovation in AI with the need for robust ethical safeguards and regulatory oversight.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized graphic depicting a human hand interacting with an AI interface, with ethical considerations (scales of justice) in the background.
GS3.trivia: India is a signatory to the 'Partnership on AI', a global initiative working to advance public understanding of AI and develop best practices for its ethical development.

GS3.headline: Government Expands Climate-Resilient Agriculture Initiatives for Small Farmers
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare announced the expansion of its climate-resilient agriculture initiatives to cover more districts and small and marginal farmers.
• The program focuses on promoting drought-resistant crop varieties, precision irrigation techniques, and climate-smart farming practices.
• It also includes components for weather-based crop insurance, advisory services, and access to climate-friendly technologies.
• The aim is to enhance the adaptive capacity of Indian agriculture to climate change impacts and ensure food security.
• Training and capacity building for farmers are central to the successful implementation of these sustainable practices.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Objectives of climate-resilient agriculture initiatives.
Key practices promoted (drought-resistant crops, precision irrigation).
Target beneficiaries (small and marginal farmers).
Role of climate change adaptation in agriculture.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges posed by climate change to Indian agriculture and the need for climate-resilient farming practices.
Analyze the various components of the government's climate-resilient agriculture initiatives and their potential impact on farmers' livelihoods and food security.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current policies in promoting sustainable agriculture and adapting to extreme weather events.
Suggest measures to further integrate scientific research and traditional knowledge for enhancing agricultural resilience.
GS3.imageDescription: A farmer inspecting a field with drought-resistant crops under a clear sky, symbolizing adaptation to climate change.
GS3.trivia: India is highly vulnerable to climate change impacts on agriculture, with millions of smallholder farmers dependent on rainfed farming, making climate-resilient strategies critical for national food security.

GS3.headline: New Government Policy Aims to Propel Startup Ecosystem in Tier-2 and Tier-3 Cities
GS3.explainer:
• The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) unveiled a new policy designed to foster startup growth beyond metropolitan areas.
• The policy offers targeted incentives, mentorship programs, and access to funding for startups in Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.
• It focuses on developing incubation centers, co-working spaces, and digital infrastructure to create a conducive environment for innovation.
• The initiative aims to decentralize economic growth, create local employment opportunities, and tap into entrepreneurial talent nationwide.
• This move is expected to democratize the startup landscape and unleash the potential of emerging innovation hubs.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Objectives of the new policy for startups in Tier-2/3 cities.
Types of incentives and support provided.
Role of DPIIT.
Benefits of decentralizing startup growth.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of fostering a vibrant startup ecosystem for India's economic growth and job creation.
Analyze the challenges faced by startups in Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities and how the new policy aims to address them.
Evaluate the potential impact of decentralizing the startup landscape on regional development and entrepreneurial talent.
Suggest policy measures to further strengthen the support system for startups, particularly in emerging innovation hubs.
GS3.imageDescription: A vibrant graphic depicting a cluster of small city skylines with upward-pointing arrows, symbolizing growth and innovation.
GS3.trivia: India is home to the third-largest startup ecosystem globally, with a significant portion of new ventures now emerging from non-metro cities.

GS3.headline: Global Cybersecurity Report Highlights Evolving Threats, Urges India to Bolster Defenses
GS3.explainer:
• A new global report on cybersecurity threats indicates a surge in sophisticated ransomware attacks and state-sponsored cyber espionage.
• The report specifically highlights India's increasing vulnerability due to rapid digitalization and expanding online infrastructure.
• It recommends bolstering critical infrastructure protection, enhancing cyber resilience, and fostering international cooperation against cybercrime.
• India is urged to invest more in cybersecurity research, develop indigenous solutions, and strengthen its legal framework for data protection.
• The findings underscore the urgent need for a multi-pronged approach to safeguard national security and economic interests in the digital realm.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
prelimsPoints: Types of modern cyber threats (ransomware, state-sponsored attacks).
India's vulnerabilities in cybersecurity.
Recommendations for strengthening cyber defenses.
Importance of data protection laws.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving landscape of cybersecurity threats and their implications for national security and economic stability in India.
Analyze the measures India needs to undertake to bolster its cyber defenses and protect critical information infrastructure.
Evaluate the role of international cooperation and indigenous capabilities in combating sophisticated cybercrime.
Examine the challenges in implementing a robust data protection framework and ensuring cyber resilience in a rapidly digitalizing economy.
GS3.imageDescription: A digital representation of a lock with binary code flowing around it, symbolizing cybersecurity and data protection.
GS3.trivia: According to some reports, India is among the top five countries most targeted by cyberattacks globally, highlighting the critical need for enhanced cybersecurity measures.
